'''Brad Fash''' (born 24 January 1996) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from Hull at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], and the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Fash was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], East Riding of Yorkshire, England.

==Career==
Fash made his Hull début on 12 July 2015 in a Super League match against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] at the [[KC Stadium]].<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/Brad-Fash|title=Brad Fash Player Stats|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=2015-11-25}}</ref>
He played 23 matches for Hull F.C. in the 2017 Super League season which saw the club go to within one game of the grand final. Fash played 17 matches for Hull F.C. in the 2020 Super League season including the clubs semi-final loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].
Fash played 25 matches for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
Fash played 12 games for Hull F.C. in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish second last on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sit-bottom-img-9630900/|title=Hull FC sit bottom of IMG performance but KR surge up as full Super League picture laid out|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
Fash played 17 matches for Hull F.C. in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 7th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/grading-each-of-super-leagues-bottom-six-in-2025-with-warrington-getting-the-lowest-marks/|title=Grading each of Super League’s bottom six in 2025 with Warrington getting the lowest marks|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
